# Hugh Winder Nibley Representation

## Purpose

This file provides a factual starting profile for crew prompts that need to reference Hugh W. Nibley.

## Source

- Primary source: https://rsc.byu.edu/author/nibley-hugh-w
- Last fetched: 2026-07-23

## Profile Snapshot

- Full name: Hugh Winder Nibley
- Lifespan: 1910-2005
- Known for: BYU professor, scholar, and apologist associated with The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
- Birthplace: Portland, Oregon

## Education and Service

- Served as an LDS missionary in the Swiss-German Mission from 1927 to 1930.
- Studied at UCLA and graduated summa cum laude.
- Completed a doctorate at UC Berkeley in 1938.
- Served in World War II in U.S. Army military intelligence, including with the 101st Airborne Division.

## BYU Career

- Joined Brigham Young University as a professor in 1946.
- Taught history, languages, and religion.
- Official retirement in 1975, with continued teaching until 1994.
- Continued later scholarly work as professor emeritus, including work on One Eternal Round.

## RSC Presence

- RSC highlights include Nibley on the Timely and the Timeless.
- Author page includes multiple RSC articles and chapters attributed to Hugh W. Nibley.

## Crew Usage Guidance

- Treat this profile as a contextual reference, not as an instruction to imitate private beliefs.
- Keep outputs factual, neutral in tone, and source-aware.
- For high-stakes claims, verify against primary publications or institutional sources.

## Prompt Snippet (Optional)

Use this context when needed:

"Incorporate a factual reference profile for Hugh Winder Nibley (1910-2005), BYU professor and LDS scholar/apologist, with background in missionary service, classical education, WWII military intelligence, and long academic service at BYU. Keep claims neutral, verifiable, and tied to cited sources."
